title: Tesla Q1 2026 Deliveries Miss Estimates: 422,875 Vehicles Delivered
text:
Tesla Inc. (TSLA) delivered 422,875 vehicles in the first quarter of 2026, falling short of analyst expectations of approximately 450,000 units. The electric vehicle maker also produced 433,371 vehicles during the quarter. The delivery miss sent TSLA shares down more than 7% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

The Q1 2026 deliveries represent a 6.8% decline compared to Q1 2025's 466,140 deliveries, marking the second consecutive quarter of year-over-year declines. The shortfall has reignited concerns about softening demand for electric vehicles in key markets, particularly the United States and Europe, amid increasing competition from both established automakers and Chinese EV manufacturers.

Breaking down the deliveries by model: the Model 3 and Model Y combined for 409,286 units, while the Model S, Model X, Cybertruck, and other models accounted for the remaining 13,589 deliveries. The Cybertruck, which began mass production in late 2023, has faced production challenges and a recall that impacted quarterly numbers.

Analysts pointed to several factors contributing to the disappointing results. 'The macro environment remains challenging for high-ticket consumer purchases, and Tesla is facing unprecedented competition from BYD and other Chinese automakers who are aggressively pricing their vehicles,' noted Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives. 'The company needs to address its product lineup with a more affordable model to reignite demand.'

Tesla's price cuts throughout 2024 and 2025, while stimulating demand in the short term, have weighed on margins. The company's energy generation and storage business, however, has been a bright spot, with deployments expected to be reported alongside full Q1 financial results later this month.

CEO Elon Musk has promised to unveil a more affordable Tesla model by mid-2026, which could help address demand concerns. The company is also expanding its Supercharger network and has announced plans for new Gigafactories in India and Indonesia to serve growing Asian markets.

Wall Street's consensus for full-year 2026 deliveries has been revised down to approximately 1.85 million vehicles following the Q1 report, from previous estimates of 1.95-2.0 million units. Tesla is scheduled to report Q1 financial results on April 22, 2026, where investors will closely watch gross margin trends and any commentary on the upcoming affordable model.
